title: rubus gremlii subsp. clusii
intro: Rubus gremlii subsp. clusii, also known as the Caucasian raspberry or Armenian raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Caucasus region and parts of Europe. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits, traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus gremlii subsp. clusii include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or jam, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
